meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap using the Antigravity project template and Claude Code. A masterclass for students building high-impact AI applications.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends by shipping production-grade AI tools, not toy projects. By leveraging the Antigravity template, you automate architectural boilerplate, allowing you to focus on high-value logic. Using Claude Code to iterate rapidly, you can transform a concept into a deployable SaaS asset in under four hours.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, the weight of a 'no experience' resume pressing down. Every job posting demands a portfolio, but every portfolio requires experience. It’s a vicious cycle that keeps talented developers in the waiting room of the tech industry, waiting for a break that never comes.

But here is the truth: the industry doesn't care about your GPA; they care about your ability to ship. By utilizing the Antigravity project template, you bypass the hours of configuration hell and jump straight into solving real-world problems. This isn't just a tutorial—it's your blueprint for building an elite-level project that forces recruiters to stop scrolling.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the foundational architecture. The Antigravity template provides a production-ready environment, ensuring your code follows industry standards for scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard scaffold. Include TypeScript, Next.js 14, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure the folder structure is optimized for AI-assisted development.

steps.title: Define the Domain Logic
steps.description: Focus on the 'why'. Use Claude Code to inject business logic that solves a specific user pain point, distinguishing your project from generic tutorials.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust service layer for an AI-powered data processor. It should handle API rate limiting, error handling, and structured JSON outputs.

steps.title: Integrate AI Middleware
steps.description: Configure the LLM orchestration layer. This is where your app becomes 'intelligent' and demonstrates your capability to work with modern AI stacks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the middleware to connect Anthropic Claude API. Ensure streaming responses are handled with a clean UI state machine.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/middleware/ai-orchestrator.ts --prompt='Implement streaming for Claude 3.5 Sonnet'

steps.title: Optimize for Production Metrics
steps.description: Add observability. Employers look for code that is monitored. Add telemetry so you can prove your app performs well in the wild.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation to the service layer. Export metrics to a local dashboard for visualization.

steps.title: Final Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Ship it. A project isn't a portfolio piece until it's live. We use automation to deploy and generate a high-quality README.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md that emphasizes the architecture, tech stack, and the specific problem this app solves for users.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run build && claude terminal 'git push origin main'

pro_tips: Always commit your work with 'atomic' commits to show a clear, logical progression of development to potential employers.
Use Claude Code to document your 'why' in the code comments, not just the 'how', to demonstrate senior-level engineering thinking.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that provides 80% of the value; ignore feature creep to ensure your project is actually shippable.
Host your project on a custom domain; it adds a layer of professionalism that free subdomains lack.
Write a 'Lessons Learned' blog post linked in your repo to show you can communicate technical complexity to non-technical stakeholders.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, share the live link on LinkedIn with a breakdown of the technical challenges you overcame during the build process.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a developer. Your breakthrough project is one prompt away. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
